name: Rosemary Chicken and Brie En Croute
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Brie cheese, rind removed, cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Unroll the crescent dough and separate it into four rectangles. Firmly press perforations to seal each rectangle.
3. Spoon about a quarter of the minced onions onto the center of each rectangle. Top with a quarter of the cheese cubes, then spread a quarter of the chicken over the cheese.
4. Fold the short ends of the dough over the chicken, overlapping slightly. Fold open the other ends about half an inch to form rectangles. Press edges firmly to seal.
5. Place the rolls seam-side down on an ungreased baking pan or cookie sheet. Make three slashes on top of each roll to allow steam to escape.
6. Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with rosemary and Parmesan cheese.
7. Bake for 21-26 minutes, or until golden brown. Garnish each sandwich with two tomato wedges and one green onion. Let stand five minutes before serving.
